steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence immediately following the underlined portion reads: 'This incident leads me to the reflection that…' That signal word 'incident' tells us the underlined sentence must function as a concrete example that supports a broader principle about imagination and reality.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It foreshadows Liang's eventual success as a writer.: focuses on writing success, which is not the point the author draws from the incident. ✗
  • Choice B — It indicates Liang's unwillingness to distinguish between reality and fantasy as a child.: inverts the relationship — imagination came first, then reality. ✗
  • Choice C — It illustrates the sense of adventure that Liang developed as a child.: adventure is incidental; the passage's reflection is about imagination foreshadowing reality, not adventure. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes an event in Liang's life that exemplifies a phenomenon.: directly supports the 'incident' that prompts the author's reflection — imagination preceding and anticipating reality. ✓
That matches Choice D — It describes an event in Liang's life that exemplifies a phenomenon..
